area_description: LLM pretraining encompasses the methods, architectures, data strategies, and optimization techniques used to train large language models from scratch on massive corpora, covering data curation, model architecture design, training recipes, scaling laws, tokenization, and efficiency improvements.

categories.name: Data Curation and Processing
categories.description: Techniques for collecting, filtering, deduplicating, mixing, and scheduling pretraining data, including synthetic data generation and multilingual corpus construction.

sub_topics.name: Data Filtering and Deduplication
sub_topics.description: Methods for filtering low-quality text, removing duplicates, and cleaning web-scraped corpora to improve pretraining data quality.

sub_topics.name: Data Mixing and Scheduling
sub_topics.description: Strategies for determining the proportions of different data sources and scheduling their presentation during pretraining.

sub_topics.name: Synthetic Data for Pretraining
sub_topics.description: Generating synthetic training data using language models or other methods to augment or replace web-scraped pretraining corpora.

sub_topics.name: Multilingual Data Curation
sub_topics.description: Methods for curating, balancing, and processing multilingual corpora for pretraining language models across many languages.

categories.name: Architecture Design
categories.description: Novel model architectures and architectural modifications for language model pretraining, including attention variants, mixture of experts, state space models, and hybrid designs.

sub_topics.name: Attention Variants
sub_topics.description: Modifications to the standard transformer attention mechanism to improve efficiency, context length, or model quality, including linear attention, sparse attention, and multi-query attention.

sub_topics.name: Mixture of Experts
sub_topics.description: Architectures that route inputs to a subset of specialized expert modules, enabling larger model capacity with reduced compute per token.

sub_topics.name: State Space Models
sub_topics.description: Non-transformer architectures based on state space models that offer linear-time sequence modeling as alternatives to attention-based models.

sub_topics.name: Hybrid Architectures
sub_topics.description: Architectures that combine multiple paradigms such as attention with state space models, or convolution with attention, to leverage the strengths of each approach.

categories.name: Training Methods and Optimization
categories.description: Techniques for training large language models, including training recipes, hyperparameter optimization, distributed training strategies, continual pretraining, and curriculum learning.

sub_topics.name: Training Recipes and Hyperparameters
sub_topics.description: Empirical strategies for configuring learning rates, batch sizes, warmup schedules, and other hyperparameters for effective pretraining.

sub_topics.name: Distributed Training
sub_topics.description: Methods for parallelizing pretraining across multiple GPUs, nodes, and clusters, including data, tensor, pipeline, and expert parallelism.

sub_topics.name: Continual Pretraining
sub_topics.description: Techniques for continuing pretraining on new data or domains without catastrophic forgetting of previously learned knowledge.

sub_topics.name: Curriculum Learning
sub_topics.description: Organizing training data in a meaningful order, such as easy-to-hard or by domain sequence, to improve pretraining efficiency and model quality.

categories.name: Scaling Laws and Efficiency
categories.description: Understanding and exploiting the relationships between model size, data size, and compute budget, as well as techniques for making pretraining more compute- and memory-efficient.

sub_topics.name: Scaling Laws
sub_topics.description: Empirical and theoretical studies of how language model performance scales with model parameters, dataset size, and compute budget.

sub_topics.name: Efficient Pretraining
sub_topics.description: Methods to reduce the compute, memory, or time required for pretraining, including mixed precision, gradient checkpointing, and architectural efficiency improvements.

sub_topics.name: Model Compression During Training
sub_topics.description: Techniques that incorporate pruning, distillation, or quantization directly into the pretraining process to produce smaller or more efficient models.

categories.name: Tokenization and Vocabulary
categories.description: Methods for designing tokenizers and vocabularies that affect how text is represented and processed during pretraining, including subword methods and multimodal tokenization.

sub_topics.name: Tokenizer Design
sub_topics.description: Algorithms and strategies for building tokenizers, including BPE, unigram, and byte-level methods, and their impact on model performance.

sub_topics.name: Multimodal Tokenization
sub_topics.description: Tokenization approaches for handling non-text modalities such as images, audio, and video within the pretraining framework.

categories.name: Pretraining Objectives
categories.description: Different training objectives and loss functions used during pretraining, including causal language modeling, masked language modeling, and alternative objectives.

sub_topics.name: Causal Language Modeling
sub_topics.description: Autoregressive next-token prediction as the primary pretraining objective for decoder-only language models.

sub_topics.name: Masked and Denoising Objectives
sub_topics.description: Objectives that mask or corrupt portions of input text and train the model to reconstruct them, including masked language modeling and span corruption.

sub_topics.name: Alternative Objectives
sub_topics.description: Novel pretraining objectives beyond standard language modeling, including contrastive learning, replaced token detection, and multi-task pretraining.

theme.name: Long Context
theme.description: Papers focused on extending context window length during pretraining or enabling models to process longer sequences efficiently.

theme.name: Multilingual
theme.description: Papers addressing pretraining of multilingual models, cross-lingual transfer, and low-resource language coverage.

theme.name: Code Pretraining
theme.description: Papers on pretraining language models specifically for code understanding and generation, including code-specific data and objectives.

theme.name: Scientific Pretraining
theme.description: Papers on pretraining language models for scientific domains, including biomedical, chemistry, mathematics, and other specialized scientific corpora.

theme.name: Analysis
theme.description: Papers where experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of baseline solutions, showing gaps and future directions.

theme.name: Benchmark
theme.description: Papers that introduce new benchmark datasets and evaluation metrics.

theme.name: Application
theme.description: Papers that show how to apply the techniques to a specific domain or task, highlighting its strengths and gaps.

theme.name: Survey
theme.description: Papers that provide a comprehensive overview of the current state of the art, including its taxonomy, advantages and limitations.
